name: TZAAR
author: Abstract Games factory
description: TZAAR (Kris Burm, GIPF project #4): a stacking capture game on a hexagonal board of hexagons (side 5, 60 playable cells, centre empty). Each player has three piece types of differing importance — Tzaar > Tzarra > Tott. Pieces slide in a straight line over empty cells to the first occupied cell (never jumping). A turn is TWO actions: a mandatory capture (slide onto an enemy stack of height ≤ yours, replacing it), then either a second capture or a stacking move that slides onto a friendly stack to combine them. You lose if you run out of any one of the three types, or cannot capture.
